name.en: Chennai City Partnership Programme - MTC Electric Buses

projects.notes: 1,000+ privately financed electric buses being deployed; Current ridership: 3.5 million daily; Target: 5.3 million daily by 2032; Fleet expansion from 3,500 to 7,500 buses; Partners: World Bank (IBRD), AIIB

budget.notes: PPP Hybrid Annuity Model; 15-year O&M contract

projects.notes: Capacity: 60 MLD; Tamil Nadu's 4th desalination plant; First outside Chennai; Industrial water supply for SIPCOT complex; Contractor: Consortium of Vishnusurya Projects, JWIL Infra, IDE Technologies India

projects.notes: 10.01 km, 17.25 m wide; Second longest in India after P.V. Narasimha Rao Expressway; First TN flyover with full-length rainwater harvesting (220 structures); Bypasses 12 traffic intersections; Reduces airport travel from 45 mins to 10 mins; Named after G.D. Naidu 'Edison of India'

timeline.completionNotes: Foundation laid January 2026; Bus terminal: 24 months; Commercial towers: 30 months

3.8 km four-lane flyover; 7 two-lane ramps (2 at Ukkadam, 2 on Palakkad road, 2 on Pollachi road, 1 on Valankulam road); Crossing time reduced to 5 minutes

timeline.completionNotes: Restoration work launched July 2025; 68 temples completed by October 2025

sources.title: The Print Hindi - Stalin launches 63 temple restoration
Social News XYZ - Temple restoration plan

projects.notes: Restoration of temples over 1,000 years old; Initial phase: 63 temples at Rs 100 crore (July 2025); Expanded to Rs 425 crore (October 2025); 68 temples completed using government, temple, and donor funds; Gold Monetisation Scheme: 1,074.123 kg purified gold from 21 temples deposited in SBI earning Rs 17.76 crore annual interest for temple development

media.photoCaption: Asia's largest fully air-conditioned integrated bus terminus

projects.notes: 40 acres; 401 bus capacity simultaneously; Asia's largest and first fully air-conditioned integrated bus terminus; Ground floor: outstation buses; First floor: city buses

budget.notes: Part of combined ₹717 Cr TIDEL Parks project

projects.notes: 5.58 lakh sq ft; G+6 floors; Expected to generate 6,000 jobs; For IT, ITES, BPOs, and startups

budget.notes: Contract value; Total Central Square cost ₹546.99 Cr

projects.notes: Phase I construction includes central prison building, 113 prison staff quarters, cement concrete road and security perimeter wall; Part of Rs 274.67 crore police and emergency services infrastructure announced on July 31, 2025; Also included police buildings (Rs 27.59 cr), fire stations (Rs 13.54 cr), prison buildings (Rs 60 lakh), forensic drug testing units (Rs 3.74 cr)

budget.notes: Phase 1; Future expansion to 165 acres

projects.notes: 45-acre Phase 1 (of 165 acres); 2+ km walking tracks; 23 themed gardens including herbal, rose, terrarium; QR-based digital plant information; Outdoor gym; On former Central Prison site at Gandhipuram

Mylapore Times - Multi-modal transport hub around Mandaveli Metro station

projects.notes: Integrated with Chennai Metro Phase 2 Corridor 3; Two multi-storeyed blocks; Tower A: ground to 7 floors for office/commercial use; Basement parking for 184 two-wheelers and 96 cars; Entry/exit structures of underground metro station inside existing MTC bus terminus; Part of Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) initiative by CMRL and TIDCO

name.en: J. Anbazhagan Flyover (T. Nagar Steel Flyover)

budget.notes: Chennai's first major steel flyover

The News Mill - Tamil Nadu CM inaugurates J. Anbazhagan Flyover
The Daily Jagran - Chennai First Steel Flyover

projects.notes: 1.2 km length; 53 steel pillars; 3,800 tonnes of steel; 6.5m above ground; 30m deep foundation; Two-way traffic; 120m entry ramp from T. Nagar bus terminus; 100m exit ramp to commercial zone; 6m service road with pedestrian footpaths; Named after late DMK senior leader J. Anbazhagan; Benefits 40,000 vehicles and 2 lakh residents daily; Reduces travel time from 45 mins to 5 mins

budget.notes: Built by ELCOT on National Highway 544

budget.notes: Phase 1A: Rs 62.42 crore + Phase 1B: Rs 50.68 crore

timeline.completionNotes: PWD invited bids for Phase 1 construction

location.city: Royapuram, Thiru-Vi-Ka Nagar, Anna Nagar (Zones V, VI, VIII)

The Hindu - Chennai residents welcome Corporation's move to revamp Otteri Nullah

projects.notes: 10.8 km canal from Padi Flyover to Buckingham Canal; Maintenance transferred from Water Resources Department to GCC in October 2024; DPR includes bridge/infrastructure improvements, desilting, pollution control, encroachment prevention, blue-green corridor creation; Chain fencing included

Devdiscourse - Tamil Nadu Boosts Rural Development with New Infrastructure
ETV Bharat - TN CM Inaugurates Infrastructure For Rural Development Dept

budget.notes: Named after freedom fighter Sankaralinganar

media.photoCaption: Road overbridge connecting Satchiyapuram to Sivakasi

sources.title: ANI News - Sivakasi residents celebrate ROB inauguration

projects.notes: Long-awaited project fulfilling decades-old demand; Eases traffic between Satchiyapuram and Sivakasi; Reduces waiting time at railway crossing; Enhances safety by reducing accidents; Named after freedom fighter Sankaralinganar who went on 77-day hunger strike for renaming Madras Province as Tamil Nadu; Residents called it a 'Diwali gift' to Sivakasi

budget.notes: Asia's largest ornamental fish market complex

media.photoCaption: Modern fish market near Villivakkam-Padi flyover

sources.title: News Karnataka - Kolathur Fish Market Overhaul
Live Chennai - Modern Fish Market Complex
Fishery News - Chennai Ornamental Fish Centre

projects.notes: Asia's largest colorful fish stall; India's second largest ornamental fish market; Over 1,000 fish species including Oscars, Gourami, sucker fish, guppies, carp, goldfish; Current monthly revenue Rs 300 crore; Developed by CMDA; Part of North Chennai Development Plan; Modern infrastructure for fish trade

budget.notes: Government's first ornamental fish trade hub

media.photoCaption: International standard ornamental fish trading center

sources.title: Live Chennai - Modern fish market complex Kolathur
News Karnataka - Kolathur's Ornamental Fish Market Overhaul
Prop News Time - Chennai's Kolathur ornamental fish trade centre
Fishery News - Chennai ornamental fish trade centre

projects.notes: Spread over 1.25 lakh sq ft (4.5 acres) in Villivakkam; 188 shops across 3 floors (64 ground, 70 first, 54 second); Each shop: 4m x 5m with 3.75m height; Large fish display tanks; 3 restaurants; Parking for 400 vehicles; Second largest ornamental fish market in India; Over 1,000 fish species traded; Current market generates Rs 300 crore monthly revenue; Part of CM Stalin's constituency development initiative; Overseen by CMDA

budget.notes: First TIDEL Park in South Tamil Nadu

media.photoCaption: G+4 floor IT park building in Meelavittan

sources.title: TIDEL Park Official - TIDEL Neo Thoothukudi
Daily Excelsior - Stalin inaugurates mini TIDEL park
Devdiscourse - New TIDEL Park Tamil Nadu
News Today - TIDEL Neo Park Tuticorin

projects.notes: Land area: 4.16 acres in Meelavittan II Village, Thoothukudi Taluk; Built-up area: 63,100 sq ft; G+4 floors with modern infrastructure; Capacity: 600 IT professionals; Fourth TIDEL Neo Park in Tamil Nadu (after Villupuram, Thanjavur, Salem); Part of Rs 234.5 crore scheme for TIDEL Neo Parks in 7 cities; CM distributed allotment letters to 2 private firms at inauguration

budget.notes: Rs 12 Cr water resources + Rs 7.25 Cr tourism

timeline.completionNotes: Announced July 22, 2025 on Rajendra Chola birthday

media.photoCaption: Historic 1000-year-old lake built by Rajendra Chola I

sources.title: Organiser - DMK sanctions Rs 19 crore to restore Chola era lake
The Print - TN CM extols greatness of King Rajendra Chola
Study IQ - Chola Gangam Lake in Tamil Nadu
News Today - New Rajendra Chola museum to be built in Ariyalur

projects.notes: Historic lake dug by Rajendra Chola I (1014-1044 CE) to commemorate northern campaign; Water resources (Rs 12 Cr): Strengthening lake banks, renovating canals, desilting 4 drainage areas; Benefits 1,374 acres of agricultural land; Tourism (Rs 7.25 Cr): Communication center, walkways, children's play area, park, security cameras; Announced on Aadi Thiruvathirai festival marking Rajendra Chola's birth anniversary; Separate Rs 22.10 Cr museum project on 10-acre plot also announced

name.en: Dr. M.S. Swaminathan Wetland Eco Park

timeline.completionNotes: Inaugurated March 7, 2025 by CM MK Stalin

media.photoCaption: First large eco-wetland park in Chennai

sources.title: ICLEI CityTalk - Chennai wetland sponge park

projects.notes: 16.63 acres on Sri Ramachandra University's Open Space Reservation land; Chennai's first wetland sponge park; Developed by CMDA with Sponge Collaborative; 600-metre boardwalk; Pollinator garden; Basketball court; Multi-use sports area; Children's play space; Elevated boardwalks; Seating areas; Landscaped channels; Named after agricultural scientist Dr. M.S. Swaminathan

The Federal - Tamil Nadu: CM Stalin inaugurates ropeway to Rathnagiriswarar hill temple

projects.notes: Part of larger HRCE temple development initiative; CM inaugurated 13 projects in 9 temples worth Rs 20.53 crore total; Temple has 1,017 steps; Ropeway faced technical snag due to high winds shortly after inauguration

budget.notes: Part of North Chennai Development Scheme

projects.notes: Lakeside park at Retteri Lake; Walking track; Natural viewing area; Children's play area; Parking and drinking water facilities; Artificial waterfall; Garden and illuminated fish tanks

budget.notes: Part of Greater Chennai Corporation sports infrastructure

sources.title: Adyar Times - CM Stalin inaugurates Rs 14.66 Cr projects

projects.notes: Upgraded facilities for cricket, basketball, volleyball, badminton, and table tennis; Part of Rs 14.66 crore sports infrastructure projects including 4 fitness parks (Rs 11.46 cr) at Minjur, Vellanur, Varadarajapuram, and Thirunageswaram; Foundation laid for Rs 255.60 crore new projects
